_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, federal AR+AZ batch)
_meta.note: Federal evidence-finalize batch (5): AR-4 Rep Westerman (R, Natural Resources Chair, Baptist, forester, AIPAC $55,630 deviation), AZ-01 R candidate Kolodin (NOT_RUNNING for AZ-01 — running for AZ Secretary of State 2026, endorsed Joseph Chaplik for AZ-01 instead; scored on AZ state rep record), AZ-01 D candidate Amish Shah (active, July 21 2026 primary frontrunner per Highground; opposed Prop 314, sponsored AZ Equality Act HB2802, Giffords-endorsed, DMI-endorsed pro-Israel-no-restrictions), AZ-01 D candidate Brian Del Vecchio (WITHDREW per Wikipedia + FEC $0 COH Aug 2025 — confidence roster_verified, no policy scoring), AZ-06 → AZ-01 Rep Schweikert (R, withdrew Sept 2025 to run for AZ Governor; down 48-18 to Trump-endorsed Andy Biggs in May 2026 polling; 2020 unanimous House reprimand for 11 ethics violations + $50K House fine + $125K FEC fine; J6 SPLIT — voted to certify AZ but objected to PA; AIPAC $206K; Iran-strike hypocrisy).

profile.religion: Baptist — denomination confirmed via Presidential Prayer Team profile; specific congregation in Hot Springs AR not publicly named in surveyed sources
profile.background: U.S. Representative AR-4 (R), in office since Jan 2015. Chairman, House Committee on Natural Resources (118th & 119th). Only forester in Congress: B.S. Biological & Ag Engineering, Univ. of Arkansas; M.F. Yale School of Forestry. 22 yrs at Mid-South Engineering, Hot Springs. AR State House 2011-2014 (Majority Leader 2013-14). Author, Resilient Federal Forests wildfire legislation. Wife Sharon; four children. AR GOP primary March 3, 2026 CANCELED (unopposed); advances to Nov 3, 2026 general vs. Dem Rus Russell III in safe-R district.

bruce-westerman.notes_set: U.S. Rep AR-4 (R), in office since 2015; House Natural Resources Chair since 2023; only forester in Congress. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th 83% / 117th 88%; NRA-PVF A; SBA Pro-Life pro-life voting record. Hard-evidence wins: cosponsor Life at Conception Act HR 431; floor UC request for Born-Alive Survivors Act; NO Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 (Dec 2022 RC 513) with on-record column 'Marriage between one man and one woman predates any government'; NO Equality Act 117th (only 3 R defectors, not Westerman); cosponsor + YES HR 734 Women's Sports; YES OBBBA (contained ECCA July 2025); YES HR 2 Secure the Border Act; YES SAVE Act HR 22 119th (cosponsor); cosponsor + YES HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance Act; NO 2nd Trump impeachment Jan 2021; NO J6 Commission establishment; joined Texas v. PA 2020 SCOTUS amicus. DEVIATION: $55,630 career-total from AIPAC/RJC/AMP pro-Israel PACs per TrackAIPAC — triggers automatic -10 RESOLUTE foreign-influence adjustment. Probable Ukraine HR 8035 YEA (NOT independently confirmed via clerk.house.gov in this sweep — flagged null on F-row to avoid fabrication). Iran/Yemen war-powers individual roll calls not located. Religion: Baptist.

4.note: OpenSecrets donor records show no Planned Parenthood / NARAL / EMILY's List PAC contributions.

4.note: NO Equality Act HR 5 (117th, Feb 25, 2021) — only 3 R defectors (Fitzpatrick, Katko, Reed); Westerman voted with party.

1.note: Party-line YEA Parents Bill of Rights HR 5 (Mar 24, 2023, RC 161, 213-208 with 5 named R defectors; Westerman not among them).

RFMA opposition column expressly cites religious-liberty and 501(c)(3) tax-status risk for marriage-as-man-woman institutions; Heritage Action 83% covers conscience-exemption / religious-liberty roll calls.

0.note: Cosponsor + party-line YEA HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (May 23, 2024).

2.note: Heritage Action 119th 83% / 117th 88% reflects consistent fiscal-conservative posture; OBBBA YEA included fiscal claw-backs.

4.note: Joined Texas v. Pennsylvania SCOTUS amicus (Dec 2020) challenging electors in PA/MI/GA/WI — election-integrity / federalism posture.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (NOT RUNNING — pivoted to AZ Secretary of State 2026; endorsed Joseph Chaplik for AZ-01)

profile.religion: Jewish — Russian-Jewish heritage (Wikipedia categorization 'Jewish state legislators in Arizona'); campaign bio uses Christian-liberty framing 'protect the freedom of Christians to share the Gospel and to practice Biblical principles'
profile.background: Arizona State Representative (LD-3 Scottsdale) since Jan 9, 2023. Chair of Select Committee on Election Integrity. Member: Federalism/Military Affairs/Elections; Judiciary; Public Safety. Member of Arizona Freedom Caucus. Georgetown undergrad; UPenn Law. Former Reagan Fellow at Goldwater Institute's Scharf Norton Center for Constitutional Litigation. PIVOTED OFFICES MAR 31 2025: NOT running for AZ-01 U.S. House — running for ARIZONA SECRETARY OF STATE in 2026 (R primary July 21, 2026); Apr 2026 NextGen poll: leads AZ GOP Chair Gina Swoboda by 9 pts. Endorsed former Rep. Joseph Chaplik for AZ-01 instead. 2020 Kraken-adjacent election lawyer for Trump campaign; State Bar of Arizona sanctioned/18-mo probation Dec 2023, ~$2,700 court costs. Husband, father of two; lives in Scottsdale.

alexander-kolodin.notes_set: AZ State Rep LD-3 (R) since Jan 2023; AZ Freedom Caucus; Chair Select Committee on Election Integrity. CANDIDACY: Announced AZ Secretary of State campaign Mar 31, 2025; NOT running for AZ-01 (which the existing scorecard record incorrectly placed him in). [2026-06 federal autopilot — scored on AZ state-rep record at federal-tier rubric] HARD evidence wins (state record applied to federal-tier rubric): Sponsored HB 2681 (in-person exam requirement for chemical abortion); YES SB 1600 born-alive infant protection; YES HB 2547 prohibiting state funds to abortion providers; NO HB 2677 (2024 repeal of 1864 abortion ban — voted to keep total ban); signed 2024 AZ GOP platform minority dissent against softening on abortion ('We're willing to kill infants to win an election... it's not worth our souls'; right-to-life-from-conception language); on-record marriage-as-man-woman position; supported 2025 ban on birth-certificate gender changes; KEY DRAFTER of Prop 314 (Secure the Border Act 2024); sponsored HCR 2001 (2026) Secure Elections Act requiring voter-ID and banning foreign election money; sponsored HB 2785 (2024) election deadline reconciliation w/ federal ECRA; sponsored 'Shall Not Be Infringed Act' (2025) repealing AZ machine gun/silencer/SBR/pipe-bomb bans; 2020 Kraken-adjacent litigation. NO HARD EVIDENCE FOUND on foreign-policy-restraint, industry-capture, federal CBDC/BBA positions — left null (state rep with no federal exposure). Religion: Jewish but campaign bio uses Christian-liberty framing.

0.note: Signed 2024 AZ GOP platform minority dissent affirming 'right to life of every human being from conception to natural death.'

1.note: YES SB 1600 (Mar 30, 2023) requiring providers to preserve life of infants born alive.

2.note: YES HB 2547 prohibiting state funds to abortion providers (AZ Title X-analog).

3.note: NO HB 2677 (Apr 2024) repealing AZ 1864 near-total abortion ban — voted to keep total ban.

0.note: On-record: 'governments should define marriage as between one man and one woman, with no other definition... legalized or supported with taxpayer or public funds'; would have preferred AZ GOP platform call Obergefell 'legally flawed.'

2.note: Supported 2025 AZ ban on birth-certificate gender amendment — protects biological-sex framework.

3.note: Campaign bio: 'protect the freedom of Christians to share the Gospel and to practice Biblical principles'; iVoterGuide religious-liberty priority.

0.note: Campaign bio pledges to 'protect the freedom of Christians to share the Gospel and to practice Biblical principles.'
Supported 2023 AZ hand-count of votes bill — paper-ballot / oppose-machines posture.

1.note: Sponsored HCR 2001 AZ Secure Elections Act (2026) requiring concurrent govt-issued voter ID for ballot drop-off.

3.note: HCR 2001 adds voter-ID requirement at mail-ballot drop-off + closes early-ballot Friday before E-Day — restricts mass-mail / drop-box framework.

4.note: HCR 2001 contains 'foreign money ban' for AZ elections — anti-Zuckerbucks/foreign-interference posture.

0.note: KEY DRAFTER of Prop 314 (2024 AZ Secure the Border Act) — criminalizes illegal entry + authorizes local-police detention/deportation.

1.note: Prop 314 authorizes local police to detain and deport — mandatory-removal mechanism.

2.note: Prop 314 cooperation-with-federal posture preempts sanctuary-city limits.

1.note: Sponsored 'Shall Not Be Infringed Act' (2025) — rationale: 'If the right to bear arms shall not be infringed, how can we have a category of weapons that are prohibited?'

2.note: Sponsored repeal of AZ bans on machine guns, silencers, short-barrel rifles, and pipe bombs — federal NFA/GCA-restriction-style state preemption.

profile.religion: Jain/Hindu mix — father Jain, mother Hindu, attended Catholic K-12 school; self-identifies in Oct 2024 Middle East policy paper as 'a Jain person' whose core value is ahimsa (non-violence). NOT a Christian.
profile.background: 2026 D candidate for AZ-01 (open Schweikert seat). ER physician, Mayo Clinic Arizona. AZ State House Rep 2019-2024 (HD-24 then HD-5). 2024 AZ-01 D nominee; lost to Schweikert 51.9%-48.1%. MD Northwestern Feinberg, MPH UC Berkeley, BA Northwestern Econ. Former team physician NY Jets; faculty Mount Sinai NYC. Indian-American (Gujarati). July 21 2026 D primary FRONTRUNNER per Feb 2026 Highground/Public Opinion Strategies poll (3:1 lead over Marlene Galán-Woods). FEC: $1.29M raised cycle 2026; $707K COH 3/31/2026.

amish-shah-az-01.notes_set: 2026 D candidate AZ-01 (open seat after Schweikert withdrew Sept 2025 to run for governor); former AZ State Rep 2019-2024; ER physician Mayo Clinic AZ. [2026-06 federal autopilot] HARD-evidence FALSEs (caucus-line D w/ some moderation): AUTHORED HB 2802 (2022) AZ Equality and Fairness for All Arizonans Act (state Equality Act + conversion-therapy ban for licensed providers; carveouts for clergy/religious institutions); COSPONSORED HB 2677 (2024) repealing 1864 abortion ban; sponsored HB 2676 abortion data-collection amendments + HB 2642 NP-prescribed emergency contraception; NO SB 1164 15-week ban; NO SB 1457 genetic-abnormality ban; NO SCR 1009 born-alive; NO SB 1600 born-alive; NO SB 1146 abortion-donor-disclosure; INTRODUCED HCR 2022 to repeal universal ESA voucher program; campaign issues page: 'Only a woman and her provider should make decisions about reproductive healthcare' + codify Roe pledge; Giffords ENDORSED 2024 ('proud to endorse'); LCV Action Fund ENDORSED 2024; AZ Choice Tracker rates 100% pro-choice. THREE NOTABLE CROSS-CURRENTS (moderate-D signals): (1) refused to support AZ Prop 314 in 2024 but didn't oppose forcefully per NRCC critique; (2) Apr 2024 Middle East paper backs 'continued U.S. aid to Israel with no additional restrictions' + Democratic Majority for Israel + Jewish Dem Council of America endorsements + 'war is necessary under a certain narrow set of circumstances' (NOT a restraint candidate); (3) campaign 'fully funding our police' language atypical for progressive D. HB 2802 contained explicit religious-institution carveouts (places of worship, religious schools, food prep). Cross-party endorsement: former GOP AZ House Speaker Rusty Bowers. NO PP/RFA/HRC 2026 endorsement located in this sweep. NO AIPAC PAC contributions to Shah located (opponent Schweikert is AIPAC-endorsed). Religion: Jain/Hindu — not Christian.

0.note: Voted NO on AZ SB 1457 genetic-abnormality abortion ban (Apr 22, 2021); campaign quote: 'Only a woman and her provider should make decisions about reproductive healthcare.'

1.note: Voted NO on SCR 1009 (Mar 18, 2021) and SB 1600 (Mar 30, 2023) born-alive infant protection / preserve-life bills.

2.note: Voted NO on SB 1146 (May 15, 2023) disclosure of corporate donors to abortion-advocacy orgs (AZ Title-X-analog opacity vote).

3.note: Voted NO SB 1164 (Mar 24, 2022) 15-week elective abortion ban; COSPONSORED HB 2677 (2024) repealing AZ 1864 abortion ban; campaign pledge to codify Roe.

4.note: Sponsored HB 2642 (2024) NP-prescribed emergency contraception; AZ Choice Tracker 100% pro-choice rating.

0.note: AUTHORED HB 2802 (2022) AZ Equality and Fairness for All Arizonans Act adding LGBTQ to AZ nondiscrimination in employment/housing/public accommodations — opposes one-man-one-woman policy framework.

1.note: HB 2802 included conversion-therapy ban for licensed medical professionals (clergy/parent carveouts) — bans counseling toward biological-sex identification.

4.note: HB 2802 IS the AZ state Equality Act analog.

0.note: INTRODUCED HCR 2022 ballot referral to fully repeal universal ESA voucher program FY2025-26 — opposes universal school choice.

1.note: HB 2802 contained explicit religious-institution carveouts (places of worship, religious schools, food prep facilities) and clergy/parental carveouts on conversion-therapy ban — moderate religious-liberty awareness above caucus norm. SOFT signal.

2.note: Campaign pledges to combat 'partisan gerrymandering' and 'eliminate dark money'; AZ legislative record includes voting-access expansion — favors expanded-access framework, not single-day in-person.

0.note: Refused to support AZ Prop 314 (Secure the Border Act, passed 63% statewide Nov 2024) when asked directly — opposes physical/legal border-enforcement expansion.
Giffords ENDORSED 2024 ('proud to endorse Amish Shah') — HARD evidence of opposition to 2A expansion.

1.note: Campaign supports universal background checks + community violence intervention + safe-storage requirements — aligned with red-flag / mag-limit / AWB framework.

2.note: DEVIATION FROM RESTRAINT: Oct 2024 Middle East policy paper backs 'continued U.S. aid to Israel with no additional restrictions'; endorsed by Democratic Majority for Israel + Jewish Dem Council of America.

3.note: Stated 'war is necessary under a certain narrow set of circumstances'; 'dismayed by the lack of international pressure to oppose and dismantle Hamas' — not an Article-I-restraint candidate.

0.note: Campaign explicitly against Big Pharma price-gouging; supports gov drug-price negotiation, $35 insulin cap, IRA expansion — anti-pharma-capture posture.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (WITHDREW from 2026 D primary; FEC C00896464 reports ceased Aug 17 2025 with $0 cash on hand; Wikipedia AZ-01 2026 lists him under 'Withdrawn')

profile.background: Administrative Law Judge, Arizona Office of Administrative Hearings (since Apr 2023). Adjunct Professor of Oral Advocacy, Thomas Jefferson School of Law (since Jul 2016). Former Deputy Director of Voter Protection, Arizona Democratic Party. Husband, father (two children: Madi and Alex); Little League baseball coach in Scottsdale. Filed Statement of Candidacy for AZ-01 D primary Nov 2024 (committee 'DEL FOR ARIZONA' C00896464); raised $15,332.78 total ($9,835 individual + $5,497.78 self-loan); LAST FEC report Aug 17, 2025 with $0.00 cash on hand. Effectively WITHDRAWN — Wikipedia 2026 AZ-01 elections page lists him under 'Withdrawn' section; campaign URL del4az.com has TLS cert errors at time of sweep.

brian-del-vecchio.notes_set: AZ-01 D PRIMARY WITHDRAWAL — Wikipedia lists him in 'Withdrawn' section for 2026 AZ-01 D primary; FEC committee DEL FOR ARIZONA (C00896464) ceased reporting Aug 17 2025 with $0 cash on hand after raising only $15,332.78 (incl. $5,497.78 self-loan); no PP/Giffords/HRC/Sierra Club/LCV endorsements logged at Ballotpedia or OpenCampaign. Limited surfaced campaign positions before withdrawal: 'CHRIST Act' children's-healthcare bill name (acronym only, not a religious-liberty signal), gov drug-price negotiation, $35 insulin cap, small-biz tax incentives, voter-protection background implies voting-access posture. ZERO scoring evidence on abortion, marriage/LGBT, school choice, religious liberty, guns, immigration policy specifics, Ukraine, Israel, Iran. SET confidence=roster_verified; NO policy cells scored — all null per evidence-first discipline. If campaign re-emerges (refiles, site reactivates), flip back to research.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (WITHDREW Sept 30 2025 to run for AZ Governor 2026; AZ-01 House seat now OPEN — Cook Toss-up; gubernatorial primary Aug 4 2026; May 2026 Emerson polling has Schweikert at 18% vs. Trump-endorsed Andy Biggs at 48%)

profile.religion: Not publicly identified — NO EVIDENCE FOUND for denomination in this sweep
profile.background: U.S. Representative since Jan 2011 (originally AZ-05; AZ-06 2013-2023; AZ-01 post-2022 redistricting). 8th term. House Ways & Means Committee. Withdrew from AZ-01 House race Sept 30 2025 to enter 2026 AZ Republican gubernatorial primary; trailing Trump-endorsed Andy Biggs 18%-48% per May 2026 Emerson poll; AZ Capitol Times labels him 'spoiler.' ETHICS HISTORY: July 31 2020 House UNANIMOUSLY reprimanded Schweikert on the House floor (rare action) for 11 ethics violations spanning Jan 2011-Nov 2017 — misuse of taxpayer Members' Representational Allowance for non-official purposes; pressured staff into campaign work on official time; failed to disclose $75K Metro Phoenix Bank loan; reported nonexistent $100K loan; ~$78K unaccounted disbursements; admitted violations; paid $50,000 House fine. Separate $125,000 FEC campaign-finance fine (PolitiFact 2022). Total ~$175K in fines.

david-schweikert.notes_set: U.S. Rep AZ-01 (R), in office since 2011; Ways & Means; WITHDREW from AZ-01 House race Sept 2025 to enter 2026 AZ gubernatorial primary (trailing Trump-endorsed Andy Biggs 18-48 in May 2026 polling). [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th 92% / lifetime 91%; Club for Growth lifetime 93% + 9x Defender of Economic Freedom; NRA-PVF A + endorsement; GOA A; HRC 0/100. Hard-evidence wins: 6x Life-at-Conception Act cosponsor (2011/13/15/17/19/21); cosponsor Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act; NO Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 (Dec 8 2022 RC 513); presumed NO Equality Act 117th (party-line); SBA Pro-Life tracked. ETHICS DEVIATION (catastrophic to economic_stewardship personal-integrity reading): July 31 2020 House UNANIMOUSLY reprimanded Schweikert on the floor for 11 ethics violations; $50K House fine; separate $125K FEC fine — rare-action material. J6 SPLIT (Jan 6, 2021): voted with Democrats to CERTIFY Arizona's 2020 electors but voted to OBJECT to Pennsylvania's electors — defensible-constitutional split per his rationale but a CROSS-PARTY deviation on the federalism / election-integrity axis. AIPAC career-total $206,443 per TrackAIPAC (RESOLUTE bracket -10 adjustment). Probable Ukraine HR 8035 vote NOT INDIVIDUALLY VERIFIED in this sweep — left null.
